channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Hollywood Actresses in Shamrock-Themed Dresses: Vintage St. Patrick’s Day Publicity Photos from the 1940s
item.description: In the 1940s, Hollywood studios frequently produced holiday-themed promotional photographs of their actresses as a way to maintain public interest and generate festive publicity. For St. Patrick’s Day...

item.title: Two Key Aspirants Pull Out from Kebbi APC Senatorial Primaries Citing Party Dynamics
item.description: Two big aspirants wey don pull out from di All Progressives Congress (APC) senatorial primaries for Kebbi State. Di primaries suppose hold on May 18, 2026. Senator Bala Ibn Na’All, wey represent Kebbi...

item.title: Greek Islands Struggle with Overtourism and Overdevelopment
item.description: Popular Greek islands such as Santorini and Mykonos are facing severe overtourism, straining both the environment and local communities. Santorini, known for its iconic cliffside villages and blue-dom...

item.title: Taiwan’s President Urges Continued U.S. Arms Support Amid China Tensions
item.description: Taiwanese President Lai Ching-te has firmly stated that Taiwan will never be 'traded away' following remarks from former U.S. President Donald Trump suggesting arms sales to Taiwan could be used as a ...

item.title: Results of APC Primaries in Nasarawa State: High-Profile Candidates Defeated
item.description: During the APC primaries in Nasarawa State on 16th May 2026, several prominent politicians lost their bids to secure party tickets for the upcoming elections. Speaker of the Nasarawa State House of As...

item.title: Thousands of Warriors Fans Celebrate Magic Round on Caxton Street
item.description: On May 17, 2026, Caxton Street in Brisbane became a sea of green and blue as thousands of New Zealand Warriors fans gathered ahead of the club’s NRL clash with the Brisbane Broncos. The atmosphere was...

item.title: KwaZulu-Natal school grounds misused as truck depot, MEC and councillor respond
item.description: During an oversight visit to a primary school on government land in Mt. Edgecombe, KwaZulu-Natal, serious misuse of school property was discovered. MEC for Public Works and Infrastructure, Martin Meye...

item.title: Home Guard Injured During Misunderstanding Over Suspected Cattle Transport in Gudimalkapur
item.description: In Gudimalkapur, Hyderabad, a home guard sustained injuries and several vehicles were damaged during a clash that erupted around midnight on Saturday. The incident began when members of a local cow vi...

item.title: A nuanced but imperfect debut exploring grief and everyday life in Kashmir
item.description: Umair Ahmed Khan’s debut novel, The Valley of Unfinished Songs, attempts to move beyond the usual political framing of Kashmir by focusing on intimate, domestic experiences. As the review notes, much ...

item.title: Affordable and Nutritious Canned Foods Recommended by a Dietitian
item.description: With the rising cost of living, it's increasingly difficult to rely solely on fresh ingredients without stretching the budget. Dietitian Susie Burrell highlights seven canned foods that are both nutri...

item.title: Sydney property sales often exceed advertised price guides by tens of thousands of dollars
item.description: Recent data from the home-buying app HOMER reveals that underquoting in Australian property markets remains widespread, with fewer than 4% of real estate listings priced within 3% of the final sale. A...

item.title: Sindh government to implement random drug tests in schools to curb rising substance abuse
item.description: KARACHI: Sindh's senior minister, Sharjeel Inam Memon, announced that the provincial government is planning to carry out rapid random drug testing in schools across the province to address the alarmin...

item.title: Sen. Chris Van Hollen Calls for Kash Patel's Removal as FBI Director, Citing Alleged Weaponization and Drinking Reports
item.description: During an appearance on NBC’s “Meet the Press” on May 17, 2026, Democratic Senator Chris Van Hollen of Maryland stated that FBI Director Kash Patel should be fired for multiple reasons. Van Hollen exp...

item.title: Taiwan president reaffirms sovereignty amid tensions with China and global diplomacy
item.description: Taiwan’s leader, Lai Ching-te, emphasised that the island will not be used as a bargaining chip in international negotiations, particularly amid ongoing tensions between China and major global powers ...

item.title: PM Shehbaz highlights Pakistan’s mediator role in US-Iran talks and political-military cooperation
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif described Pakistan’s role as a key mediator between the United States and Iran as a proud moment in the country's history. In an interview with The Sunday Times, he credi...

item.title: Paper Rex clinches fourth VCT Pacific Stage 1 title; invy secures second Filipino regional championship
item.description: In the 2026 VALORANT Champions Tour (VCT) Pacific: Stage 1, Paper Rex continued to dominate the competitive esports scene by securing their fourth Pacific trophy. The team displayed consistent perform...

item.title: Journalists Criticize Trump Administration Over Potential Cuba Military Threat
item.description: A recent report by Axios, citing an anonymous U.S. official, suggested that Cuban leaders have discussed hypothetical plans to use military drones against U.S. interests in the event of hostilities. T...

item.title: Research Highlights Watermelon’s Nutritional and Heart Health Benefits
item.description: Recent research indicates that watermelon is more than just a hydrating summer fruit—it may play a significant role in improving overall diet quality and supporting cardiovascular health. Studies usin...

item.title: Optimal Greening launches sustainable water project in Ikorodu North communities
item.description: Optimal Greening don officially start Project SWIFT, wey be sustainable water initiative, for Isiwu and Eginrin Ekun communities for Ikorodu North, Lagos. Dis project dey aim to give people clean wate...

item.title: Gauteng Schools Struggle Under R600 Million Municipal Debt, Threatening Services
item.description: Gauteng's education system is under severe pressure as schools face a municipal debt approaching R600 million. The crisis is disrupting electricity and water supply, with hundreds of schools at risk o...
